Q01.question: Is the pattern spec complete, named, and unambiguous?
Q01.evidence: P-34 has a full named spec: 'Empirical-Threshold-Sweep-over-ML-Classifier'. Spec includes trigger, decision gate (4 conditions), evidence basis (KAPPA v3.5->v3.6, 14x12 grid, governance_clear 82.6%->100%), thresholds (STRONG=0.22, BLENDED=0.18), NIST and EU AI Act refs. Unambiguous and complete.

Q02.question: Is the trigger condition clearly defined and non-overlapping with existing patterns?
Q02.evidence: Trigger: 'confidence calibration gap exists in routing or scoring component'. 4-gate decision check distinguishes from ML classifier (bounded, interpretable, auditable, reversible). No existing P-01-P-33 pattern covers threshold sweep methodology. Minor overlap risk with P-27 (adaptive weighting) is explicitly resolved: P-34 is the calibration prerequisite FOR P-27, not a substitute.

Q03.question: Is the tradeoff profile documented with at least 2 pros and 2 cons?
Q03.evidence: Implicitly documented via decision gate: pros = bounded, interpretable, auditable, reversible. Cons implicit in gate conditions (requires stable feature space, feasible grid search, direct metric). Full explicit tradeoff block not present in P-34 spec as registered — minor gap vs P-01-P-10 format standard.
Q03.verdict: PASS (minor gap noted: explicit tradeoff block recommended for merge)

Q04.question: Is there empirical evidence supporting the pattern's effectiveness?
Q04.evidence: Strong: KAPPA v3.5->v3.6, 14x12 grid sweep, governance_clear 82.6%->100% improvement, BLG-01 closed. Concrete quantitative evidence with specific grid dimensions and metric delta. This is the strongest evidence basis of any P-27+ pattern.

Q05.question: Does the pattern have a reference implementation or artifact?
Q05.evidence: Evidence basis references KAPPA v3.5->v3.6 and S034 grid sweep. No explicit `components/` file path listed in P-34 spec (compare: P-27 refs `components/KAPPA/dynamic_weight_router.py`). Pattern is validated through the KAPPA calibration work but lacks a standalone reference implementation path. Recommend adding ref path before merge.

Q06.question: Are all cross-references to other patterns accurate and by pattern number?
Q06.evidence: Interaction map correctly lists: P-34 -> P-27 (calibration prerequisite), P-34 -> P-30 (attestation required), P-34 -> P-03 (contract re-assertion). All by number. Differentiation doc lists P-34 in Layer 7 Router Calibration alongside P-27 and P-28. Consistent across all files.

Q07.question: Is the pattern layer assignment correct and non-conflicting?
Q07.evidence: Assigned to Layer 7 (Router Calibration) in NDR_REGISTRY_DIFFERENTIATION.md alongside P-27 and P-28. This is correct: P-34 is the calibration METHOD that produces thresholds consumed by P-27 (routing) and P-28 (pipeline). Layer assignment is non-conflicting and semantically accurate.

Q08.question: Does the pattern avoid re-implementing existing patterns?
Q08.evidence: P-34 is compositionally distinct from all P-01-P-33. P-27 consumes threshold outputs; P-34 produces them. P-28 pipelines routing; P-34 calibrates it. No duplication with safety/sentinel (P-29), attestation gate (P-30), or runtime safety (P-31-P-33). COMPOSE registration was correct.

Q09.question: Is NIST / regulatory mapping present and accurate?
Q09.evidence: NIST: Measure mapped — correct, threshold calibration is a measurement activity. EU AI Act: Art.9 mapped — correct, Art.9 covers risk management system requirements including accuracy. Mapping is accurate. Minor gap: Art.13 (transparency) could be argued for interpretability benefit but is not required.

Q10.question: Is the registration session and authorship traceable?
Q10.evidence: Registered: S066 · 2026-05-30 · Agent Amethyst. COMPOSE classification present. Session history in SESSION_ANCHOR.md, ENSEMBLE_ROSTER.md S066 session notes, and CO_ORCH_QUEUE Q-S066-P34-REG all confirm. Full provenance chain.

Q11.question: Would this pattern be recommended for adoption in a new governance-grade system?
Q11.evidence: Yes. The empirical-before-ML principle is a high-value governance heuristic: it lowers complexity, improves auditability, and keeps the feature space interpretable at small-to-medium scale. The 4-gate decision check provides a clear adoption boundary. Recommended with one advisory: explicit tradeoff block and reference implementation path should be added to P-34 spec at merge time.
Q11.verdict: PASS (advisory: finalize spec format at merge)

open_blgs.description: Explicit tradeoff block (pros/cons format matching P-01-P-10 standard) not present in P-34 spec as registered
open_blgs.resolution: Add explicit tradeoff block to P-34 spec section during Phase 3 merge execution

open_blgs.description: Reference implementation path not explicitly listed in P-34 spec (e.g., components/ file path)
open_blgs.resolution: Add ref path to P-34 spec during Phase 3 merge execution

scoring_summary.attestation_note: A-TIER with 2 minor open BLGs. Both resolvable during Phase 3 merge execution. Pattern is structurally sound, empirically validated, correctly layered, and recommended for canonical promotion. Conditional attestation is valid per P-30 gate.

ratification_gate.note: This attestation is authored by Amethyst via dynamic orchestration on behalf of Apogee. Per P-09 Triumvirate governance, this document is PENDING ENDER RATIFICATION. Ender confirmation promotes to Apogee-signed artifact and closes PM-07.
